title: Immunofluorescence protocol for FFPE human post-mortem brain sections to detect alpha-synuclein and tau pathology
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"fbcgr","text":"This protocol details the method for preparing and staining human formalin-fixed, paraffin embedded post mortem brain tissue to detect alpha-synuclein and tau pathology using immunofluorescence.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"5nt5e","text":"Formalin-Fixed Paraffin-Embedded (FFPE) sections of the human midbrain and cortical regions were cut at 6um with a Leica HM325 rotary microtome and mounted on Series 2 adhesive microscope slides.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Place slides with FFPE human brain tissues on a slide rack and bake in a 60 °C oven for 01:00:00

2. Continue using the slide rack, submerge slides in the following solution to de-paraffinize: a. HistoChoice: 2 x 00:07:00 b. 100% ethanol: 2 x 00:03:00 c. 95% ethanol: 00:03:00 d. 70 % ethanol: 00:03:00 3. Submerge in MiliQ/ultrapure water for 00:03:00 . 4. Tap gently sideways on flat surface covered with paper towels to remove excess water.

5. Submerge slides in 70% Formic Acid for 00:20:00 . 6. Wash in MiliQ/ultrapure water for 2x 00:05:00 . 7. Remove slides from the rack and submerge slides directly into 0.01 Molarity (M) sodium citrate buffer ( pH 6 ) and incubate sections in a programmable antigen retrieval cooker 8. Let the pressure cooker reach its peak of 121 °C before gradually cooling for a totalof 02:00:00 . 9. Wash with MiliQ/ultrapure water for 00:01:00 , followed by washing with 1 X PBS for 00:05:00 .
10. Prepare 0.1% Sodium borohydride (NaBH 4 ) in 1xPBS for 00:30:00 of quenching. The solution must always remain chilled in ice. 11. Wash in 1x PBS for 2x 00:05:00
12. Add Human FC blocker in 1X PBS with a ratio of 1:50, incubate slides with Human FC blocker at Room temperature for 00:05:00

Component A (Background Suppressor) may become turbid or form a gel at 4 °C this does not affect performance. Warm the buffer to room temperature or 37 °C until clear (light blue) and completelyliquid before use. 13. Add enough TrueBlack‱Background Suppressor to completely cover sample. Room temperature 00:30:00 14. Remove the Background Suppressor and add IF Blocking Buffer (home-made). Room temperature 01:00:00

5. Prepare 150 ul per sample of primary antibody solution consisting of selected primary antibody diluted in home-made blocking buffer. %s %s 16. Prepare humidified chamber and remove Blocking buffer by tapping on paper towel. 17. Add primary antibody diluted in Blocking buffer and incubate for 48:00:00 in 4 °C

1. Wash slides in PBST 3x 00:10:00 . Slides must be protected from light from this step. 2. Prepare 150 ul per sample of secondary antibody solution consisting of selected secondary antibody diluted appropriately in home-made blocking buffer. %s %s 3. Incubate sample in secondary antibody in Room temperature for 02:00:00 4. Wash slides in PBST 3x 00:05:00 , followed by 1x PBS 1x 00:05:00

5. Prepare conjugated antibodies using Zenon‱ labeling kits within 00:30:00 before applying the antibodies. %s %s %s %s 6. Incubate sample in conjugated antibodies in Room temperature for 02:00:00 7. Wash slides in 1x PBS 3x 00:05:00

8. Prepare 150 ul per sample of 1xTrueBlack‱ Lipofuscin Autofluorescence Quencher in 70% ethanol. 9. Tap slides to paper towels to remove excess washing solution and then place in humidified chamber. 10. Add diluted TrueBlack‱ solution and incubate for 30-60 seconds. 11. Rinse slides in 1xPBS 3x 00:05:00

12. Remove as much moisture without drying tissue using Kimwipes 13. Retrieve Mounting with EverBrite‱ Mounting Medium to warm at Room temperature before dispensing approximately 10 - 20 ml on slides. 14. Place coverslip gently on the slides and wait for the Mounting Medium to dry before applying the perimeter of the coverslip with Biotium’s CoverGrip‱ Coverslip Sealant. 15. Store in a protected slide boxaway from light at 4 °C
creator: courtney.wright Wright
